name: stray-japan-govdocs
description: Repo-local Codex plugin for answering user requests with Japanese government whitepapers, statistics, budgets, cases, official policy documents, and temporary source caches.

interface.shortDescription: Turn user requests into Japan government-backed background, evidence, KPI, e-Stat, Project LINKS, real-estate-library data, budget, case, chart-data, citation, cache, and whitepaper workflows.
interface.longDescription: A repo-local Codex plugin for request-driven use of Japanese government whitepapers, policy documents, and selected open datasets. It dispatches broad requests in the same turn, separates neutral background from proposal synthesis, supports supplied claims with official evidence, distinguishes KPI discovery from chart-source tracing and e-Stat retrieval, grades ownership and priority evidence, traces budget lifecycle and official cases, analyzes Project LINKS and Real Estate Information Library data with explicit fallbacks, audits citations, manages schema-validated temporary caches, and briefs named documents using conservative official-source boundaries.
defaultPrompt: Route a broad or mixed Japan government-backed request and execute the selected specialist workflows in the same turn.
Build official government background for a theme,企画書,営業資料,or strategy memo.
Find official evidence and source links for a claim.
Check whether a theme is treated as a current or rising government priority.
Map a theme to relevant ministries, agencies, whitepapers, and policy contexts.
Adapt a business idea or service into proposal-ready government context without overstating support.
Audit whether existing whitepaper or government-document citations are official, current, and correctly described.
Find KPI and outcome-indicator candidates for a Japan policy or social issue; use e-Stat analysis separately for table retrieval.
Search, retrieve, and analyze e-Stat official statistics with table IDs, metadata, filters, units, and caveats.
Inventory, inspect, download, profile, or analyze MLIT Project LINKS datasets from G-Spatial Information Center with source and quality cautions.
Retrieve and analyze MLIT Real Estate Information Library data such as land prices, transaction prices, urban planning, nearby facilities, and disaster-risk layers.
Trace a policy issue to budget programs, administrative review sheets, and funding signals.
Find official case examples from Japanese whitepapers and government documents.
Trace a specific whitepaper chart or statistic back to its source data where possible.
Temporarily cache Japanese government whitepaper PDFs, HTML, spreadsheets, and source indexes under tmp/japan-govdocs/ with manifest records.
Brief a named Japanese government whitepaper, annual report, chapter, or official policy document.
When reading PDFs or attachments, download only necessary official documents into tmp/japan-govdocs/ and record them in manifest.jsonl.
